item.title: v1.7.0
item.description: Sign in with Apple in onboarding (with manual fallback). Real cloud sync via Supabase — your rock follows you to any Mac you sign into. Share button rewritten so it works reliably (no more random misses). Mac + Windows download buttons on the website. Windows app scaffold landed in the repo (Tauri). Quit glitches fixed. For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.7.0
item.description: Glitch fixes (popover now sized correctly + cleaner Quit). Windows beta waitlist on the website. Windows app scaffold landed in repo (Tauri + TypeScript). For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.6.0
item.description: First-launch onboarding asks for your name + (optional) rock name. Share URLs now include your byline. Share page shows '"" — by ' and lets you click any grain to see when it was earned. For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.5.3
item.description: Real app blocking now: blocked apps get force-quit on activation during a focus session (graceful terminate, then force-terminate if it survives). The lockout overlay sits at screenSaver level so it shows over fullscreen games. Block list is dormant outside of sessions. For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.5.2
item.description: Share button is now front-and-center in the popover, right next to Claim Grains, always visible when you have a rock to share. For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.5.1
item.description: Share-rock URLs actually work now (use hash-fragment form instead of path so Cloudflare's .html-stripping doesn't break them). For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.5.0
item.description: Pending grains now stay in escrow until you press Claim N grains. The pour-in animation got way slower and more rewarding — each grain lands with a yellow halo and overshoot scale. Pixels → grains everywhere. For Gravy. 🪨

item.title: v1.4.9
item.description: Zero give-up penalty (your pending pixels always pour in, even if you stop early). New 'Share your rock' button copies a public link — anyone can view your rock in their browser. Brand-new WidgetKit widget: pin your boulder to Notification Center. Polished tag editor, cinematic release ceremony, parallax mountain range, more realistic boulder rendering everywhere.

item.title: v1.4.8
item.description:
• Harder app blocker. Tabbing to a blocked app now hides it on EVERY activation (no cooldown — keep tabbing, it keeps vanishing). Three hides over 0.5s in case the app tries to restore itself.
  • Fullscreen lockout overlay. Big red "🪨 BLOCKED" panel pops up center-screen with the pixel cost. Auto-dismisses after 2 seconds. You can't miss it.
  • Honest scope note: macOS gates real OS-level app blocking behind FamilyControls entitlements Apple only grants to approved parental-control apps. This is the strongest non-destructive blocking an indie app can ship.

item.title: v1.4.7
item.description: • 2-minute give-up grace period. Started a session by accident? You have 120 seconds to give up at zero cost. After that, give-up forfeits only your pending pixel escrow — never pixels already on the rock. The "floor of 5" crumble is gone.

item.title: v1.4.6
item.description:
• Menubar countdown. During a committed focus session, the menubar 🪨 now shows the live countdown next to it ("25:00 → 24:59 …"). Open-ended sessions show elapsed time. Hidden when not focusing.
  • Stats tab + Calendar heatmap. Settings → Stats. Three big tiles (Total Focus / This Week / Streak), a GitHub-style year heatmap tinted by each day's dominant tag, and per-tag hour bars.
  • Positive streak. Days in a row with ≥1 minute focused. No streak guilt.

item.title: v1.4.5
item.description:
• Stats tab. Settings → Stats. Three big tiles (Total Focus / This Week / Streak), a GitHub-style calendar heatmap of your year, and per-tag bars showing where your hours went.
  • Calendar heatmap. 53 weeks × 7 days. Each cell tinted by the day's dominant tag color, intensity scaled by minutes focused. Hover any day → date · minutes · pixels · top tag.
  • Positive streak. Days in a row with ≥1 minute focused. No streak guilt — just a milestone counter.

item.title: v1.4.4
item.description:
• Countdown preview. Picking a duration (15m / 25m / etc.) now previews the countdown immediately — you see 15:00 before pressing Focus, then it counts down from there. Previously showed 00:00 → looked broken.
  • Pour-in animation is way tougher. Zoom-in cranked from 1.12× → 1.30×. Stagger slowed (0.06–0.18s per pixel) so each pixel is individually visible. Each pixel pops in with overshoot scale (0.05 → 1.45 → 1.0) and a yellow halo flash that fades over 0.5s. Each landing reads like a small impact.

item.title: v1.4.3
item.description:
• Pixels pour in when you stop. Pixels now accumulate INVISIBLY during focus. The popover shows a "+N px pending" gold capsule next to the momentum pill so you still see momentum. Press Stop and the boulder zooms in 12%, all your pending pixels pour in one-by-one with a stagger + fade, then it zooms back out. One satisfying reveal per session.
  • Per-pixel dates. Every pixel now records the exact moment it was earned. Click a pixel and the inspector overlay shows the earned-at timestamp ("2 hr ago · 8:34 PM" / "Mon · 8:34 PM" / "May 12, 2026 · 8:34 PM" depending on age). Foundation for a calendar heatmap.
  • Template menubar icon. The menubar 🪨 is now a system-tinted template (matches Bear's paw, Apple's symbols). Auto-flips black/white for light/dark mode. Cleaner look in the menubar, same Boulder identity.
  • Give-up forfeits escrow. Give up during a committed session and you lose the entire pending escrow (those pixels never land). Floor of 5 — short-tap-Focus-then-Give-Up still costs you. The button label updates dynamically.
  • Completion bonus rides the pour. When a committed timer naturally hits 0, the bonus pixels join the pending escrow and ride the same pour-in animation. One reveal, not two.

item.title: v1.4.2
item.description:
• Photo-real rock. Boulder's surface now has multi-octave value noise, procedural cracks following a 2D wave field, vertical weathering streaks, moss/lichen flecks in the shaded quadrant, ambient occlusion at the edges, and a cast shadow on the ground.
  • Stage vignette. The rock now sits in a subtle inset radial gradient — feels like the boulder is in a space, not floating.
  • Refined popover header. Tier name + "N px to next tier" subtitle, dedicated momentum capsule (green glow dot, label, multiplier), and the pixel count as a quieter monospace.

item.title: v1.4.1
item.description:
• Critical crash fix. BoulderShape.computeCells trapped on a signed-int overflow the first time pixels were emitted. Every focus session was dying within seconds. Hash math now uses safe UInt32 truncation.
  • Slower, more deliberate growth. 1 pixel per 5 minutes at 1× momentum (was 1 per 12 seconds). Mountain tier is now a real 6-month commitment, not a weekend grind. Momentum tier multiplier still ramps 1× → 3× during long sessions.
  • App blocker now pushes back. Switching to a blocked app during focus now hides it, brings Boulder forward, opens the popover, and crumbles pixels. macOS won't grant indie apps the FamilyControls entitlement to true-block apps — this is the closest we can get.
  • Menubar boulder fix. Logo now renders thread-safely via NSImage drawingHandler with pre-resolved colors. No more sub-pixel speck.
  • Notifications in foreground. Crumble banners now show even while Boulder is frontmost.

item.title: v1.4.0
item.description:
• Real-looking rock. Pixels now snap to a dense dome silhouette — every cell is integer-grid-aligned, packed edge-to-edge, lit from above with a 20-shade granite gradient and subtle vein inclusions. No more scattered rubble.
  • Connected silhouette. The boulder is a single coherent mass at every pixel count, growing wider and taller in proportion as you focus.
  • Per-tag rock tints. Tags now produce 20 smoothly-interpolated shades capped at low saturation — Granite/Slate/Basalt/Jade/etc. each read as tinted stone, never as a vivid hue.
  • Automatic migration. Existing rocks are reshaped on first launch — tag and session attribution preserved, only the (x,y,shade) positions update.
  • Pixel-perfect rendering. Renderer rounds baseline + cell size to integer pixels to kill sub-pixel anti-aliasing seams.

item.title: v1.3.0
item.description:
• You make your own tags. First launch starts empty; you create the categories that matter to YOU with a name, emoji, color, and description.
  • Commitment sessions. Pre-select 15m / 25m / 45m / 1h / 90m / Open. Committed sessions count DOWN and earn a small bonus on completion. Giving up early chips 25% of session pixels off Boulder.
  • Session descriptions stamped onto every pixel you earn. Click a pixel later → see what you were doing.
  • Pixel inspector — tap your rock to surface the session that grew the pixel cluster you tapped (tag, description, date, gave-up flag).
  • Tag library in Settings → Tags. Hue slider for color, palette preview, edit/delete.
  • Force-quit-safe commitment. Quitting Boulder mid-commitment counts as a give-up — no backdoor escape.
  • Honest note: macOS won't let an indie app system-lock you out of your computer. Boulder builds commitment through pixel stakes instead.

item.title: v1.2.0
item.description:
• Momentum tiers. The longer you focus in one session, the more pixels per minute you earn. 1× warming up, ramps to 3× after an hour of continuous focus. Stop = reset. No punishment for breaks — just a reward for staying deep.
  • You always see your rock. Even with zero pixels, the popover shows a starter pebble silhouette. Auto-scales so a 5-pixel Boulder and a 5000-pixel Mountain both feel substantial.
  • Real app icon. Procedurally generated pixel boulder on a night-sky backdrop. Visible in Finder, the DMG, the About panel.
  • Momentum readout. "Rolling × 1.5×" in the popover header during focus sessions.

item.title: v1.1.0
item.description:
• Boulder shows up in your menubar. The icon is the actual rock, growing pixel by pixel as you focus — no more emoji placeholder.
  • App blocking. Pick the apps that break your focus (Slack, Twitter, Discord). Switching to one during a focus session chips 3 pixels off Boulder and posts a notification. 8-second cooldown so a single mis-tab doesn't punish you twice.
  • Settings actually work. Cmd+, opens a real settings window with launch-at-login, blocked apps, and an About tab.
  • Crumble flash. Boulder shakes and shows a red -3px counter when your focus breaks.
  • Popover polish. Live "Focusing" indicator, blocked-apps strip, cleaner tier label.

item.title: v1.0.0
item.description:
• Boulder is born. A pet rock for your focus that can't die — it just grows, one pixel at a time, every minute you focus.
  • Five focus types — code, write, read, audio, design — each stamps a different texture into your Boulder.
  • Mountain Range gallery for retired Boulders. Release a Mountain-sized Boulder into the sea; it joins your skyline.
  • Menubar only. No Dock icon, no nagging, no streak guilt. Truly chill.
